meta.description: Statement review follow-up where a review_summary is present in audience_context. All 3 versions must reference the actual findings and must not invent data not in the summary.

strategy.audience_context: Electrical contractor. Review summary: statement shows a mix of card-present and card-not-present transactions with inconsistent batch settlement timing, which may be causing avoidable downgrade fees. No specific dollar amounts confirmed yet.
strategy.pain_point_hypothesis: Downgrade fees from mixed transaction types and late batch settlement.
strategy.trust_angle: Reference review findings transparently; invite a call to discuss the details.
strategy.proof_point: Inconsistent batch settlement and mixed card-present/not-present transactions noted in review.
strategy.cta: Could we find 20 minutes this week to walk through what I found?
compliance_notes: Reference only findings in the review summary
Do not claim specific dollar savings
required_inclusions: Reference actual review findings from audience_context
strategy.reasoning: Review summary present; findings should be referenced without inventing data.

selected_skill_definitions.instructions: Reference actual review findings. Propose a call. Do not invent data.
Be transparent. Build credibility through specific observations.
Reference electrical field payment patterns where relevant.
Only reference findings stated in the review summary. No dollar amounts unless confirmed.

expected.expected_subject_behavior: Subjects reference the review or findings; none are generic cold-outreach subjects
expected.expected_body_behavior: All 3 versions reference the actual findings in audience_context (batch settlement, mixed transaction types); none invent new data points
expected.expected_cta_behavior: Each version invites a call to discuss the findings
expected.pass_fail_notes: All versions must reference batch settlement and mixed transaction types from the review summary. Invented findings not in the summary are a hard fail.
